title: Lemon Chicken
tagline: Bright, zesty, and weeknight easy. The pan sauce is the star.
description: Seared chicken cutlets in a glossy lemon butter sauce with capers and fresh herbs. Restaurant quality, home kitchen simple.
ingredients: 4 boneless skinless chicken breasts, pounded to 1/2 inch thick
steps: Season chicken with salt and pepper. Dredge in flour, shaking off excess.
Heat olive oil and 1 Tbsp butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Cook chicken 4-5 minutes per side until golden and cooked through. Remove to a plate.
Reduce heat to medium. Add garlic and cook 30 seconds.
Pour in chicken broth and lemon juice. Scrape up browned bits. Simmer 2 minutes.
Stir in remaining butter, lemon zest, and capers. Swirl until butter melts and sauce thickens slightly.
Return chicken to pan. Spoon sauce over and cook 1 minute.
Top with parsley and serve immediately.
notes: Pound the chicken: Even thickness = even cooking. Use a meat mallet or rolling pin.
Hot pan: Get the pan hot before adding chicken for proper browning.
The sauce: Work quickly once you start—it comes together in minutes.
Serve with: Rice, orzo, roasted potatoes, or crusty bread to soak up the sauce.
Make it piccata: This is essentially chicken piccata. Add more capers if you love them.
